data.title: BMO Capital Initiates Buy on Parker Hannifin Amid Structural Upgrade and Enhanced Earnings Power
data.description:
, an analyst from BMO Capital, has initiated a new Buy rating on Parker Hannifin (PH).

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

BMO Capital has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Parker Hannifin’s structural repositioning and earnings power. The firm highlights Parker’s transition into a higher‑quality industrial franchise, with a larger share of its business now tied to secular growth markets and recurring aftermarket revenue that support more durable cash flows and a premium valuation multiple.

BMO Capital’s rating is based on expectations that both cyclical and structural drivers can support upside to estimates and the current share price. Improving indicators in shorter‑cycle end markets, ongoing margin expansion from a disciplined operating culture, and a robust M&A and capital allocation track record underpin their view that the company can continue to outperform despite its strong historical run.

In another report released on March 23, Citi also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $1,092.00 price target.

data.title: iBio (IBIO-610): Validated Activin E Mechanism, Strategic Trial Positioning, and Attractive Risk-Reward Support a Buy Rating
data.description:
Patrick Dolezal, an analyst from LifeSci Capital, maintained the Buy rating on Ibio. The associated price target remains the same with $5.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Patrick Dolezal has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to IBIO-610’s differentiated mechanism and favorable positioning. He views the recent WVE-007 results, despite investor disappointment, as validating the broader strategy of targeting activin E, supporting potential applications in combination with incretin therapies, in patients with obesity and type 2 diabetes, and in maintenance settings after initial weight-loss treatment.

He also emphasizes that iBio can leverage extensive emerging clinical data, including Wave’s and Arrowhead’s programs, to refine trial design, patient selection by BMI and visceral fat, and optimal treatment lines. With an IND for IBIO-610 anticipated in Q4 2026, a multi-year cash runway, and a clearly defined development path, he believes the risk-reward profile remains attractive at current valuation levels, supporting a Buy recommendation.

In another report released on March 18, Chardan Capital also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $5.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: BMO Capital Initiates Buy on ITT as a Durable Growth Platform Driven by Accretive M&A, Margin Expansion, and Strong Commercial Execution
data.description:
ITT (ITT) has received a new Buy rating, initiated by BMO Capital analyst, .

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

BMO Capital has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight ITT’s transformation into a durable growth platform. The firm points to ITT’s shift from a purely efficiency-focused operator to a company pairing operational discipline with strong commercial execution, driving 5%+ organic growth, new product success, and favorable pricing on higher-value solutions.

BMO Capital also emphasizes the upside from accretive M&A, margin expansion, and a resilient balance sheet. Recent acquisitions, including SPX FLOW, have exceeded expectations and support double-digit revenue growth, while management still sees several hundred basis points of margin improvement ahead. With moderate leverage, diversified end-market exposure, and visibility into 2026+ earnings, BMO views ITT as well-positioned to surpass its long-term financial targets.

data.title: BMO Capital Maintains Hold on IDEX Amid Solid Fundamentals but Limited Near-Term Upside and Cyclical Exposure
data.description:
IDEX (IEX) has received a new Hold rating, initiated by BMO Capital analyst, .

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

BMO Capital has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including solid fundamentals but limited near‑term upside. The firm sees IDEX delivering low‑ to mid‑single‑digit organic growth, gradual margin improvement, and disciplined capital deployment that can support a double‑digit EPS growth profile over time, but believes a stronger macro and industrial cycle is needed to fully unlock that potential.

At the same time, the stock has already rerated higher, outperforming the broader industrial group and now trades in line with multi‑industry peers rather than at its historical premium. With roughly half of IDEX’s portfolio still exposed to cyclical end markets and consensus expectations already reset lower, BMO Capital prefers to wait for clearer evidence of a sustained growth inflection and positive earnings revisions before moving to a more bullish stance.

In another report released yesterday, TipRanks – Anthropic also reiterated a Hold rating on the stock with a $203.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Krystal Biotech: Valuation Reflects Pipeline Optimism Amid Dosing and Efficacy Risks, Justifying a Hold Rating
data.description:
Kalpit Patel, an analyst from Wolfe Research, has initiated a new Hold rating on Krystal Biotech (KRYS).

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Kalpit Patel has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to Krystal Biotech’s current valuation and pipeline risk profile. He views the ocular programs KB801 and KB803 as scientifically sound but notes that the move to more frequent dosing hints the initial regimens may not have been optimal, raising questions about tolerability and real-world practicality.

Patel is also cautiously encouraged by early molecular signals from the cystic fibrosis candidate KB407, yet he stresses that meaningful and durable lung-function improvement and long-term safety still must be demonstrated. Given that a significant portion of Krystal’s roughly $7B market value already reflects anticipated pipeline success, he believes the upside is limited relative to the risks and prefers to wait for further clinical de-risking or a more attractive entry point before becoming more constructive on the shares.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
